name: Highland Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering checking, savings, CDs, loans, mortgages, and digital banking services to consumers and small businesses.
description_long:
Highland Bank is a federally regulated financial institution with FDIC insurance backing, indicating deposits are protected by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank operates both consumer and commercial divisions, serving individual customers and small to medium-sized businesses through a network of physical locations and digital channels.

The bank provides a comprehensive suite of financial products including Kasasa checking and savings accounts with cash-back and interest-earning features, CDs and IRAs, debit and credit cards, personal loans, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), mortgages (including purchase, refinance, and construction options), and investment planning services. For businesses, Highland offers commercial checking and savings accounts, business loans, SBA lending, cash management solutions, and dedicated commercial banking support.

Highland distinguishes itself through partnerships with Kasasa, offering rewards-based checking accounts, and by providing specialized mortgage products like construction mortgages and HELOC options with promotional rates. The bank emphasizes digital capabilities with a comprehensive Digital Banking Suite including bill pay, card management, Zelle transfers, and mobile app access. They also highlight fraud prevention tools, financial education resources, and community involvement programs.

Highland Bank appears to be a legitimate, federally-chartered institution with transparent regulatory identifiers (NMLS ID #478369, Routing #091916378). However, the website provides limited detail on specific rates, fees, loan terms, or minimum balance requirements. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly or visit locations for detailed pricing and eligibility information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with cash-back rewards
Kasasa savings accounts with interest-earning benefits
Digital banking with bill pay, card management, and Zelle
ATM network and mobile wallet access
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Kasasa checking accounts earn cash back and higher interest rates
Comprehensive mortgage options including construction loans and HELOC specials
Digital Banking Suite with Zelle, bill pay, and mobile app functionality
Dual focus serves both consumers and small/commercial businesses effectively
Fraud prevention tools and financial education resources available
Multiple account types (checking, savings, CDs, IRAs) in one institution
cons: Website lacks specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for comparison
Limited transparency on loan terms, minimum balances, or eligibility requirements
No clear information on customer service availability (hours, phone support responsiveness)
best_for: Small business owners seeking SBA lending and commercial banking relationships
Homeowners looking to leverage equity through HELOCs or mortgages
Consumers wanting rewards-based checking with Kasasa benefits
diagnosis: Highland Bank is best for customers seeking a full-service bank with FDIC protection, rewards-based checking, and mortgage/HELOC options, particularly small business owners and homeowners. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal rate and fee transparency, requiring direct contact with the bank for specific pricing and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
